name: Oko ↝ Lonely Polar Bear
description:
♡ 𝐬𝐧𝐨𝐰 𝐛𝐞𝐚𝐫 ♡

Oko stumbles upon his first ever potential mate~ you!

First intro is FemPov. Second into is AnyPOV.

{{user}} can be anything. (An Arctic animal demihuman, a human photographer, etc...)

I've been trying to make a Polar Bear Demi for AGES, but genning thick men is sooo difficult for no reason lol.

It's funny cause my idea was basically like the animated short that just came out!

It's amazinggg. Take a peek~

SNOW BEAR - A Hand-Drawn Animated Short Film (4K) by Aaron Blaise (YouTube)

🚨 PLEASE READ 🚨

The US government is gutting the Endangered Species Act. Four dockets aim to strip protections from vulnerable animals.

Comment Deadline: December 22, 2025 (DUE THIS SUNDAY!) on Regulations.gov

Please take action! It doesn't take long. Every unique comment counts. You can do it anonymously!

1. Endangered and Threatened Species: Listing Endangered and Threatened Species and Designating Critical Habitat. Docket Number: FWS-HQ-ES-2025-0039

↝ This rule changes the criteria for adding species to the protected list. While science remains the legal baseline for listing, the proposal removes the explicit ban on mentioning economic impacts during the process, allowing the government to publish cost reports alongside biological data. It also raises the "burden of proof" for protecting species threatened by climate change by requiring that both the threat and the animal's response be proven "likely" rather than just "predictable."

2. Endangered and Threatened Species: Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants. Docket Number: FWS-HQ-ES-2025-0029

↝ It removes the "Blanket Rule," which currently gives "threatened" species the same automatic protections as "endangered" ones. Under the new plan, newly listed threatened species would have no default protections; instead, the government must write a unique, custom rule for every single species-- which can take years.

3. Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Interagency Cooperation Regulations. Docket Number: FWS-HQ-ES-2025-0044

↝ This governs how federal agencies (like the Bureau of Land Management) consult with wildlife experts before approving projects like pipelines or dams. The proposal narrows the definition of project "effects" to only those that are direct and "reasonably certain" to occur. It also removes the explicit authority for the government to require "offsets," where a company must restore habitat elsewhere to compensate for the damage its project causes.
creator: AstarionApproves
